title: chenopodium ambrosioides subsp. retusum
intro: Chenopodium ambrosioides subsp. retusum, also known as Mexican tea or epazote, is a plant species in the amaranth family native to Mexico and Central America. The medicinal properties of Mexican tea include its ability to repel insects, aid in digestion, and exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Mexican tea include its potential use in treating flatulence, indigestion, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its ability to reduce stress and anxiety. Mexican tea can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing its leaves in hot water, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make salves, ointments, and other remedies.
